description.en: Check your child's grades, attendance, report cards, and teacher comments. PowerSchool is the district's student information system — it's where all academic records live.

accessHtml.en:
Log in through the EUSD website. You'll need a parent account, which your child's school office creates for you.

How to get access

  1. Contact your child's school office and ask for a PowerSchool parent account
  2. They'll give you a username and temporary password
  3. Go to the PowerSchool parent page and click the login link
  4. Sign in and change your password

Tips

  • If you have multiple children in EUSD, you can link all of them to one parent account — ask the school office
  • The mobile app is also available, but the web version works on any device

description.en: Stay up to date with school news, district announcements, and emergency alerts. Rooms (by Thrillshare) sends push notifications straight to your phone so you never miss important updates.

accessHtml.en:
Download the free Rooms app or log in on the web.

Get the app

  1. Download Rooms from the App Store (iPhone) or Google Play (Android)
  2. Open the app and search for "Escondido Union School District"
  3. Tap "Follow" on your child's school and the district
  4. Allow push notifications when prompted — that's how you'll get alerts

Web access

Don't want the app? You can also log in at escondidousdca.id.thrillshare.com to see the same news and announcements in your browser.

description.en: View daily breakfast and lunch menus for all EUSD schools. Check nutrition facts, see allergen info, and know what's being served before your child heads to school.

accessHtml.en:
EZSchoolLunch is a website — no app download or login required.

How to view menus

  1. Go to ezschoollunch.com/p/Escondido/home
  2. Select your child's school
  3. Browse the daily and weekly menus

Good to know

  • All EUSD students eat free breakfast and lunch every day — no application needed
  • Menus show allergen information for common allergens
  • If your child has a specific dietary need or allergy, contact your school's cafeteria manager

description.en: Find out which EUSD school your child is assigned to based on your home address. Just enter your street address and the tool shows your neighborhood elementary and middle school.

accessHtml.en:
The School Site Locator is a free online tool — no login or app needed.

How to use it

  1. Go to the School Site Locator
  2. Enter your home address
  3. The tool will show you which elementary school and middle school serve your address

This is especially useful if you're new to Escondido or moving to a new neighborhood within the district.

description.en: Report a safety concern anonymously. Say Something (by Sandy Hook Promise) lets students, parents, and community members submit tips about potential threats, bullying, or someone in crisis — 24/7, completely anonymous.

accessHtml.en:
Say Something is a free anonymous reporting system run by Sandy Hook Promise.

How to submit a tip

  1. Visit the Say Something website
  2. You can also download the Say Something app
  3. Submit your tip — it's completely anonymous
  4. Trained crisis counselors review every tip 24/7

When to use it

  • Threats of violence (to self or others)
  • Bullying or cyberbullying
  • Someone who seems to be in crisis
  • Weapons or dangerous situations

In an emergency, always call 911 first.
